name: always_disjoint
reason: Would mean any_set always returns false, making the overlap check useless

name: zero_mask_nonempty_view
code:
proof fn phi_2_zero_mask_nonempty_view(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        forall|i: int| 0 <= i < 8 ==> cm.mask[i] == 0usize,
    ensures
        exists|i: int| cm@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: A mask with all zero words should have an empty view set; a non-empty view would corrupt disjointness checks

name: never_disjoint
reason: Would mean any_set always returns true, making it impossible to detect non-overlapping masks

name: view_always_empty
reason: Would collapse every CommitMask view to the empty set, making any_set trivially return false regardless of actual bits

name: full_mask_self_disjoint
code:
proof fn phi_5_full_mask_self_disjoint(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        forall|i: int| 0 <= i < 8 ==> cm.mask[i] == !0usize,
    ensures
        cm@.disjoint(cm@),
{
}
reason: A mask with all bits set must overlap with itself; self-disjointness here would indicate the view spec is broken
